cards.q: ما هو عنوان الفصل الذي يتناوله الكتاب؟
cards.a: Chapter 11: Large Objects (الصفحة 2)

cards.q: ما هي الأنواع التي يتم تناولها في أول هدف من أهداف الفصل؟
cards.a: LOB Types (الصفحة 3)

cards.q: كيف يمكن إنشاء جدول يحتوي على عمود LOB؟
cards.a: Creating a Table with a LOB Column (الصفحة 3)

cards.q: ما الموضوع المتعلق بقراءة الملفات الخارجية المذكور في محتويات الفصل؟
cards.a: Reading BFILEs (الصفحة 3)

cards.q: ما هو الموضوع الذي يغطيه الهدف الأول المتعلق بالـ LOBs؟
cards.a: أنواع الـ LOB (LOB Types) (الصفحة 6)

cards.q: ما هي العناصر التي يتم توضيحها في الهدف الثاني المتعلق بـ LOBs؟
cards.a: مؤشرات ومفهرسات وقطع الـ LOB (Illustrating LOB Locators, Indexes, and Chunks) (الصفحة 6)

cards.q: ما الذي يغطيه الهدف الثالث فيما يتعلق بالـ LOBs؟
cards.a: جدول يحتوي على عمود LOB (Table with a LOB Column) (الصفحة 6)

cards.q: ما هو الموضوع الذي يتناوله الهدف الرابع في سياق الـ LOBs؟
cards.a: قياس المساحة المستهلكة من الـ LOB (Measuring LOB Space Consumed) (الصفحة 6)

cards.q: What type of data are LOBs suited to storing?
cards.a: Large and unstructured data, such as text, log, image, video, sound, and spatial data (الصفحة 8)

cards.q: Which LOB type is used for storing character documents like big text files or XML files?
cards.a: CLOB (Character large object) (الصفحة 8)

cards.q: What distinguishes the BFILE data type from other LOBs?
cards.a: It stores binary file large object on the filesystem outside of the database; it is read-only and not covered by Oracle security, backup, or recovery mechanisms (الصفحة 8)

cards.q: What are examples of semi-structured data mentioned in the text?
cards.a: Document files such as json, XML documents or word processor, and log files (الصفحة 9)

cards.q: What SQL data types are ideal for storing large amounts of unstructured binary data?
cards.a: The BLOB data type (Binary Large Object) and the BFILE data type (Binary File object). (الصفحة 10)

cards.q: In Internal LOBs, what is the smallest unit of allocation for a LOB, which is made up of one or more database blocks?
cards.a: A chunk. (الصفحة 12)

cards.q: When querying a table with an Internal LOB, what components does the database use to locate the appropriate LOB chunks?
cards.a: The LOB locator and associated LOB index. (الصفحة 12)

cards.q: What information does the LOB locator for a BFILE store in an External LOB setup?
cards.a: The directory path and file name on the OS. (الصفحة 12)

cards.q: When is it recommended to create a Secure File LOB in a separate tablespace from the table data?
cards.a: If the LOB segment will require different storage characteristics such as size and growth patterns (الصفحة 13)

cards.q: What are the benefits of partitioning a LOB?
cards.a: Partitioning helps with balancing I/O, maintenance, and backup operations (الصفحة 14)

cards.q: Which SQL statement is used to modify the storage characteristics of a partitioned LOB?
cards.a: ALTER TABLE ... MODIFY PARTITION (الصفحة 15)

cards.q: What are the methods mentioned for partitioning a LOB table?
cards.a: Partitioning can be done by LIST, RANGE, or HASH (الصفحة 14)

cards.q: What are the two types of underlying LOB architecture that Oracle distinguishes between?
cards.a: Basic Files and Secure Files (الصفحة 16)

cards.q: Name three enhancements included in Secure Files compared to Basic Files LOBs.
cards.a: Encryption, Compression, and Deduplication (الصفحة 16)

cards.q: What must a Secure Files LOB be stored in?
cards.a: A tablespace using ASSM (Automatic Segment Space Management) (الصفحة 16)

cards.q: Which database parameter controls whether a Secure Files file can be used and defines the default LOB architecture for your database?
cards.a: DB_SECUREFILE (الصفحة 18)

cards.q: What underlying LOB architecture is the default, and what is recommended for creating a LOB?
cards.a: The default underlying LOB architecture is Secure Files; it is recommended to create a LOB as a Secure Files. (Page 20)

cards.q: Prior to Oracle Database 12c, what two components are created for each LOB column?
cards.a: A LOB segment and a LOB index. (Page 20)

cards.q: What is the "deferred segment creation feature" regarding LOBs?
cards.a: The LOB segment and index are not created until a record is inserted into the table. (Page 20)

cards.q: Which view can be used to verify if a LOB is stored in-row?
cards.a: The USER_LOBs view. (Page 21)

cards.q: When Oracle creates a LOB column, what two main structures does it create?
cards.a: A LOB segment (which stores the data) and a LOB index (which keeps track of where the chunks of LOB data are physically stored). (الصفحة 22)

cards.q: According to the text, when is an initial segment for a table or LOB column created?
cards.a: An initial segment is not created until you insert a row into the table (deferred segment creation). (الصفحة 22)

cards.q: What clause must be used in the CREATE TABLE statement to specify a separate tablespace for a LOB segment?
cards.a: The `LOB...STORE AS` clause. (الصفحة 24)

cards.q: Which view can be queried to display information regarding LOB segments and LOB index names?
cards.a: `user_lobs` (or DBA/ALL/USER_LOBS). (الصفحة 22)

cards.q: If an LOB segment requires different storage characteristics (such as size and growth patterns), what is recommended to manage its storage?
cards.a: Create the LOB in a separated tablespace from that of the table data. (الصفحة 25)

cards.q: What must be true about the tablespace containing the Secure File LOB, and what is the default LOB architecture?
cards.a: The tablespace must be ASSM managed; the default LOB architecture is Secure Files. (الصفحة 25)

cards.q: What SQL command creates a table with a Secure Files LOB column named `patch_desc` in the `lob_data` tablespace?
cards.a: `SQL> create table patchmain( patch_id number ,patch_desc clob) lob(patch_desc) store as securefile (tablespace lob_data);` (الصفحة 25)

cards.q: Which view can be queried to verify a LOB's architecture?
cards.a: The `USER_SEGMENTS` view, by querying segment_name, segment_type, and segment_subtype. (الصفحة 25)

cards.q: With the SecureFiles architecture, what options are no longer needed to be specified when creating LOBs?
cards.a: CHUNK, PCTVERSION, FREEPOOLS, FREELIST, and FREELIST GROUPS. (الصفحة 28)

cards.q: What is the benefit of using a partitioned table that has a LOB column?
cards.a: It lets you spread a LOB across multiple tablespaces, helping with balancing I/O, maintenance, and backup and recovery operations. (الصفحة 28)

cards.q: What statement is used to change the storage characteristics of a partitioned LOB column after it is created?
cards.a: ALTER TABLE ... MODIFY PARTITION statement. (الصفحة 29)

cards.q: Which edition is required for Partitioning and Advanced Compression?
cards.a: Oracle Enterprise Edition. (الصفحة 30)

cards.q: If a table is created with a LOB column and no tablespace is specified, where is the LOB created by default?
cards.a: In the same tablespace as its table. (الصفحة 32)

cards.q: What statement can be used to move a LOB column to a tablespace separate from that of the table?
cards.a: `ALTER TABLE...MOVE...STORE AS` statement. (الصفحة 32)

cards.q: What is the basic syntax for moving a LOB column to a new tablespace?
cards.a: `alter table <table_name> move lob(<lob_name>) store as (tablespace <new_tablespace>);` (الصفحة 32)

cards.q: Why is it recommended to store the LOB in a separate tablespace from the rest of the table data?
cards.a: Because the LOB data have different growth and storage requirements and are best maintained in their own tablespace. (الصفحة 32)
